key_insight: MOSAIC addresses modality-incremental continual learning by stabilizing new sensor representations, separating modality statistics, and recovering plasticity to reduce forgetting in Parkinson’s gait assessment.
engineering_relevance: The framework is relevant for clinical sensing systems that must adapt to new wearable modalities over time without access to full historical multimodal data.
method: Modality-Specific Warm-Up to prevent unreliable cross-modal distillation from a “Toxic Teacher.”
Statistics-decoupled MSBN to isolate sensor-specific statistics while sharing semantic features.
Curriculum-guided repulsive objective for Plasticity Recovery to preserve old knowledge and regain modality-specific capacity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on three multimodal Parkinson’s gait datasets, with improved final performance and reduced forgetting.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful tuning of warm-up, distillation, and repulsive-loss schedules, especially when modality distributions shift across sites or devices.
paper_title: MOSAIC: Modality-Specific Adaptation for Incremental Continual Learning in Parkinson's Disease Gait Assessment
